title: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking, urban (% of urban population)
definition: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking, urban is the proportion of urban population primarily using clean cooking fuels and technologies for cooking. Under WHO guidelines, kerosene is excluded from clean cooking fuels.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include United States, Uruguay, Trinidad and Tobago, while the lightest include South Sudan, Burundi, Liberia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the urban clean cooking access pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Urban clean cooking access separates modern city infrastructure from broader energy access.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Clean cooking fuels access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Rural clean cooking access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
GNI per capita, PPP can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Settlement maps reward checking dense city-states and large rural countries.
Urban and rural shares are mirror-image clues.

reviewNotes: Batch 3 QA: strong access pattern but close to clean-fuels and rural clean-cooking maps.
Practice-only until the access cluster is tuned.
